# WI-QUIC-CRT-0018 - QUIC CRT trim and Native AOT compatibility work item

## Summary

Add trim and Native AOT compatibility to the shipped Incursa.Quic package on the supported net10.0 target, and prove that the package remains consumable through PackageReference under PublishTrimmed and PublishAot.

## Requirements Addressed

- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0120

## Design Inputs

- ARC-QUIC-CRT-0018

## Planned Changes

- Enable trim and Native AOT analyzer coverage for the supported net10.0 library build.
- Audit the public API and internal QUIC helpers for reflection, runtime code generation, and other trim or AOT incompatible patterns.
- Add a downstream PackageReference consumer smoke path that publishes the package under trimming and Native AOT.
- Add a requirement-home proof that keeps the package boundary, analyzer warnings, and downstream publish results explicit.
- Keep unsupported dynamic-extension or plugin-based consumer models out of scope.

## Out of Scope

- unsupported plugin or reflection extension models
- multi-target packaging beyond the supported net10.0 slice
- .NET Framework support
- benchmarks

## Verification Plan

Build the library with trim and Native AOT analyzers enabled, then publish a downstream PackageReference consumer with PublishTrimmed and PublishAot and fail the slice on any trim or AOT warnings or runtime regressions. Re-run the repo-local SpecTrace validation checks after the artifact updates.

## Completion Notes

Optional implementation notes, deviations, or follow-up items.
